rows.notes: Section header clearly written: 'Soluta Ordinaria' (distinct, centered-ish). No monetary amounts on this header line.

Abbreviated Latin label reads 'Act. schol. et Officiaries' (interpreted as 'Act. schol. & officiaries'). Amount 559£ 13s 7d is visible though ink slightly darker/fainter in places; digits align with the £/s/d column lines.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payments to scholars and officiaries, amounting to £559 13s 7d.

rows.notes: There is a prominent struck-through label (illegible due to cross-out). To the right the numeric total reads '1332 10 6' with a small quarter-pence mark (farthing) visible — interpreted as 0.25. Because the label is struck through the textual description is unreadable; numeric reading is reasonably clear but the cross-out reduces confidence.
Label 'Summa Receptorum' is legible. Numeric reading '1575 6 0' is clear and aligned; all currency columns within valid ranges.

Label 'Solutorum' (sum of payments) is clear. Amount repeated here '1332 10 6' with a visible farthing mark (interpreted as 0.25) matching the earlier struck-through numeric total; columns align well.

Reads 'Restant Debita a Dno Computante' (remaining debts to the gentleman computing). Numeric value at right appears as '242 15 5' with a three-quarter mark (three farthings, interpreted as 0.75). Slight smudge on fractional mark but shape consistent with '3q'.
Date line 'pro 21. Die Novembris 1775' is a clear datation/heading; no amounts on this line.
Phrase 'Computus comprobatus per Nos' (computation checked by us) is legible and clearly functions as a header/introduction to signatures.

First signature/endorsement line reads 'Tho Bray' with a following office word resembling 'Rectorum' or 'Rectorum'. Handwriting is cursive but legible enough; interpreted as a signature heading rather than a monetary row.
Second signature reads 'H. Richards' and the office looks like 'Subrectorem' (sub-rector). Penmanship is slightly lighter but readable.
Third signature appears 'Tho: Stinton' with an office reading 'Decanum' (dean). Slight uncertainty on surname spelling ('Stinton' plausible).

Signature reads something like 'Fran Upton' followed by abbreviated office which is difficult to parse (looks like 'Vicar' or 'Vary Sec.'). Handwriting light and abbreviations obscure exact office — included as header/signature with low confidence.

This signature line is heavily abbreviated and partly overwritten; reads approximately 'Jo: Sorrande' with 'Burs:' (bursar) following but both name and office are uncertain due to overwriting and faded ink.
Final signature looks like 'J. Toms' followed by heavy scribbles/abbreviations. The line is partly crossed and contains marginal annotations; read as a signature/header with low confidence.
